or **Macabi București**, later known as **Ciocanul**, was a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") sport club, representing the [Jewish community](/wiki/History_of_the_Jews_in_Romania "History of the Jews in Romania"), akin to the famous [Hakoah Vienna](/wiki/SC_Hakoah_Wien "SC Hakoah Wien"). Named after the [Maccabees](/wiki/Maccabees "Maccabees") and centering on [football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") competitions, it was the first [Jewish](/wiki/Jew "Jew") side to send a player, the goalkeeper [Samuel Zauber](/wiki/Samuel_Zauber "Samuel Zauber"), to the [FIFA World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_World_Cup "FIFA World Cup") (at its [1930 edition](/wiki/1930_FIFA_World_Cup "1930 FIFA World Cup") in [Uruguay](/wiki/Uruguay "Uruguay")).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football club

Maccabi București  
Ciocanul  
Dinamo A

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Full name | Maccabi București |
| Short name | Maccabi |
| Founded | 15 May 1919 |
| Dissolved | 1948 |
| Ground | [Calea Dudeşti](/wiki/Dude%C8%99ti,_Bucharest "Dudești, Bucharest") |
|  | |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | **[Home colours](/wiki/Kit_(association_football) "Kit (association football)")** | **[Away colours](/wiki/Away_colours "Away colours")** | | |
|  | |

**Maccabi** or **Macabi București**, later known as **Ciocanul**, was a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") sport club, representing the [Jewish community](/wiki/History_of_the_Jews_in_Romania "History of the Jews in Romania"), akin to the famous [Hakoah Vienna](/wiki/SC_Hakoah_Wien "SC Hakoah Wien"). Named after the [Maccabees](/wiki/Maccabees "Maccabees") and centering on [football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") competitions, it was the first [Jewish](/wiki/Jew "Jew") side to send a player, the goalkeeper [Samuel Zauber](/wiki/Samuel_Zauber "Samuel Zauber"), to the [FIFA World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_World_Cup "FIFA World Cup") (at its [1930 edition](/wiki/1930_FIFA_World_Cup "1930 FIFA World Cup") in [Uruguay](/wiki/Uruguay "Uruguay")).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maccabi_Bucure%C8%99ti&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Maccabi București was founded in 1919 by a Jewish entrepreneur at a time when representatives of the [ethnic minorities](/wiki/Minorities_of_Romania "Minorities of Romania") of [Greater Romania](/wiki/Greater_Romania "Greater Romania") established distinctive football sides (another such example was [Elpis Constanța](/wiki/Elpis_Constan%C8%9Ba "Elpis Constanța"), which was owned by a [Greek-Romanian](/wiki/Greeks_in_Romania "Greeks in Romania") businessman).[[1]](#cite_note-1) In 1925, a women's seven-a-side [handball](/wiki/Field_handball "Field handball") team was inaugurated as a branch of the Maccabi club, but, like all Romanian teams of the time, only played [exhibition games](/wiki/Exhibition_game "Exhibition game") (usually, as a prelude to competitive football matches).[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Before the 1940–1941 season, at a time when Romania adopted [antisemitic](/wiki/Antisemitism "Antisemitism") policies, the club was expelled from official competitions. It reemerged in 1945 at the end of [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") and kept its name before merging with another club to form *Ciocanul*.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

For the 1946 season, the club was coached by Hungarian coach [Béla Guttmann](/wiki/B%C3%A9la_Guttmann "Béla Guttmann"), who went on to coach many prominent European and South American teams of the 1940s and 1950s.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Due to food shortages, Guttmann insisted his salary be paid in vegetables.[[5]](#cite_note-auto8-5)[[6]](#cite_note-name-6) He subsequently walked out on the Romanian club after a director attempted to intervene in team selection.[[7]](#cite_note-auto12-7) German journalist Hardy Grune believed that he was frustrated with the corruption in the Romanian soccer world.[[6]](#cite_note-name-6)

As Ciocanul, the club played in two seasons in [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"). In May 1948, it merged with [Unirea Tricolor București](/wiki/FC_Unirea_Tricolor_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Unirea Tricolor București"), to create the present-day major Romanian club [Dinamo](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București"), which was administrated by the [Ministry of the Interior](/wiki/Minister_of_Interior_and_Administrative_Reform_(Romania) "Minister of Interior and Administrative Reform (Romania)").[[8]](#cite_note-mpstad-8) During the 1947–1948 season they remained two separate clubs: *Dinamo A* (*Ciocanul*) and *Dinamo B* (*Unirea-Tricolor*).[[8]](#cite_note-mpstad-8)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maccabi_Bucure%C8%99ti&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Honours")]

**[Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"):**

:   * **Winners (0):**, *Best finish:* 7th [1946–47](/wiki/Divizia_A_1946%E2%80%9347 "Divizia A 1946–47")

**[Liga II](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II"):**

:   * **Winners (1):** [1934–35](/wiki/1934%E2%80%9335_Divizia_B "1934–35 Divizia B")
    * Runners-up (1): [1938–39](/wiki/1938%E2%80%9339_Divizia_B "1938–39 Divizia B")
